title: Stratford & Tyburn Rd / Five Ways (day trip)

main_route.excerpt: We went to Stratford, but there was no theatre matinée or cinema. Simon took us to a metal spinner & Powlett & Loach.
We drove on to Tyburn Rd to see "Executive Decision", which we enjoyed
afterwards we had a delicious supper at J Jays at 5-ways, although we were wearing old clothes

boundary_flags: Page A_0177_R has mixed_start=true: the opening text ('sketch because it is a view of the west wing...') belongs to the preceding trip (Farnborough Hall). The May 28th entry begins with '12 we set off to the south'.
summary: A single-day outing on 28 May 1996 from Birmingham south to Stratford-upon-Avon, where the family visited a metal spinning workshop (Powlett & Loach), before driving to a cinema on Tyburn Road to see 'Executive Decision'. The day ended with supper at J Jays restaurant at Five Ways, Birmingham.
extraction_notes: Page A_0177_R is a boundary page (mixed_start=true): the first paragraph describes Farnborough Hall (Long Walk, obelisk, temples) which belongs to the preceding trip. The May 28th Tuesday section starts cleanly mid-page. 'Stratford' is Stratford-upon-Avon (the obvious destination for a family day trip south of Birmingham). 'Tyburn Rd' is a road in north/east Birmingham (Erdington/Erdington area) where there was a cinema; the film 'Executive Decision' was released in 1996. Five Ways is the well-known junction in Edgbaston, Birmingham. Peter rested in the van at Farnborough Hall (preceding trip text) — not relevant to this trip. No crossings or excursions.
